South Africa 0-1 Canada

stories.headline: Why Vaibhav Suryavanshi should not make his India debut in the 1st T20I vs England — Indian Express
stories.what: The Indian Express has published a considered argument against handing Vaibhav Suryavanshi his India debut in the first T20I of the England series. The piece weighs the prodigious youngster's potential against the risks of premature exposure at the international level in a high-profile opening match. The debate around Suryavanshi's readiness is one of the most talked-about selection discussions in Indian cricket ahead of the series.
stories.lens: The question of when to hand a gifted young talent his debut directly affects India's T20 strategy and the career trajectory of one of cricket's most hyped teenagers.
stories.article:
Vaibhav Suryavanshi, the teenage batter who has taken the cricketing world by storm with his exploits in domestic and franchise cricket, is at the centre of a selection debate ahead of India's T20I series against England. The Indian Express has made a case that the youngster should not be handed his international debut in the opening match of the series.

The argument, as presented, centres on the pressure and scrutiny that accompanies a high-profile debut against a quality England attack in the very first game of a five-match series. While Suryavanshi's talent is undisputed, being thrown in at the deep end in a series opener — with the spotlight at its brightest — may not be the ideal circumstance for a first cap, the piece contends.

Suryavanshi has attracted enormous attention for his age-defying performances, but managing the expectations around him will be crucial to his long-term development as a cricketer. A more measured introduction later in the series, when conditions and match context might be more favourable, could allow him to settle without the weight of a nation watching his every move from ball one.

The debate encapsulates a broader tension in Indian cricket between the excitement of backing young talent immediately and the wisdom of protecting promising players from premature pressure. How the selectors and team management handle Suryavanshi's introduction will be closely watched.
key_facts: Indian Express argues against Vaibhav Suryavanshi debuting in 1st T20I vs England
Suryavanshi is one of Indian cricket's most hyped young talents
Five-match T20I series against England is the context for the debate

stories.headline: Push Sports: How a ₹1 lakh cricket academy became 'India's New Playground'
stories.what: YourStory profiles Push Sports, a cricket academy that started with a seed investment of ₹1 lakh and has grown into what is being described as 'India's New Playground' for grassroots cricket development. The story charts the academy's rise from a modest beginning to a broader platform that aims to democratise cricket coaching and talent discovery across India. It reflects a growing entrepreneurial wave in Indian sports infrastructure.
stories.lens: With cricket being India's dominant sport, affordable academies that widen access to professional coaching can genuinely shape where the next generation of players comes from.
stories.article:
Push Sports, a cricket academy launched with just ₹1 lakh, has emerged as an unlikely but compelling story of grassroots sports entrepreneurship in India. YourStory's profile charts how the venture — built on the belief that quality cricket coaching should not be the preserve of the privileged — grew from a tiny seed into what its founders now bill as 'India's New Playground'.

The academy's model is centred on accessibility and scalability, seeking to bring structured coaching to cricketers who might otherwise lack the resources or proximity to established training centres. Starting from a single location with minimal capital, Push Sports has expanded its footprint and approach over time, demonstrating that high-quality sports infrastructure does not have to begin with deep pockets.

The story resonates at a time when Indian cricket is producing remarkable talent from Tier-2 and Tier-3 cities, with players from outside the traditional metropolitan hubs increasingly finding their way into franchise leagues and domestic competitions. Academies like Push Sports are part of the ecosystem making that possible.

For entrepreneurs and sports enthusiasts alike, the Push Sports story is a case study in vision meeting execution — and a reminder that the infrastructure of Indian cricket's future is being built not just by the BCCI, but by passionate individuals willing to start small and dream large.
key_facts: Push Sports cricket academy started with ₹1 lakh
Branded as 'India's New Playground' for grassroots cricket
Story profiles democratisation of cricket coaching in India

stories.headline: 'Tilak Varma was playing for himself': Srikkanth blasts India batter after series defeat
stories.what: Former India captain Krishnamachari Srikkanth has sharply criticised Tilak Varma, accusing the young batter of prioritising personal milestones over team needs following a series defeat. Srikkanth's comments reflect a growing debate about Tilak Varma's shot selection and intent in high-pressure situations. The criticism from such a prominent voice in Indian cricket is likely to put the batter under significant scrutiny ahead of the England T20I series.
stories.lens: When a former India captain publicly questions a young player's attitude after a series loss, it signals real selection and performance pressure that matters for India's T20 future.
stories.article:
Krishnamachari Srikkanth, one of India's most celebrated former openers and a member of the 1983 World Cup-winning squad, has launched a pointed attack on Tilak Varma, accusing the young middle-order batter of playing for himself rather than for the team during India's recent series defeat.

Srikkanth's remarks, carried by The Times of India, reflect wider concerns within Indian cricket circles about Tilak Varma's approach at the crease. The allegation that he prioritised personal milestones or individual strokeplay over the team's run-chase or defensive requirements cuts to the heart of what Indian cricket expects from its middle-order batters.

The timing of the criticism is significant. With the five-match T20I series against England imminent, Tilak Varma will be desperate to silence his critics with performances that clearly demonstrate team-first intent. The backlash from a figure as prominent as Srikkanth adds pressure that the 22-year-old will need to handle maturely.

The episode also reignites broader questions about how India's emerging middle-order batters are being developed and whether the pressure of expectation is shaping their in-game decision-making in the wrong direction. Srikkanth's blast is a wake-up call that will not be easily ignored.
key_facts: Srikkanth accused Tilak Varma of 'playing for himself'
Criticism follows a series defeat for India
Tilak Varma faces scrutiny ahead of the England T20I series

stories.headline: India vs England T20I series 2026: Full schedule, squads, venues and how to watch
stories.what: With the India vs England five-match T20I series 2026 imminent, fans are seeking complete details on the schedule, venues, squad compositions, and live streaming options. The series is a marquee bilateral T20 contest between two of world cricket's biggest sides. Coverage details have been confirmed across broadcasters and digital platforms, making it straightforward for fans across India to follow the action.
stories.lens: For millions of Indian cricket fans, knowing exactly when, where and how to watch the series live — especially on phones and TV — is practical and essential information.
stories.article:
The India vs England T20I series 2026 is set to capture the attention of cricket fans across the country, with a five-match contest scheduled between two of the format's most powerful teams. Full details on the schedule, complete squads, match venues, IST timings, and live streaming options have now been confirmed and widely reported.

The series promises to be a compelling contest, with England arriving as a side renowned for their aggressive T20 brand of cricket, while India boast a deep and talented pool of players across batting, bowling and the all-round department. Squads from both sides have been announced ahead of the first match.

Fans can follow the series through confirmed live broadcast and streaming arrangements, ensuring that viewers on television and digital platforms are catered for. The IST timings for each match have been detailed, allowing supporters across time zones within India to plan their viewing.

With five matches to be played across multiple venues, the series is expected to be a high-quality battle that will also serve as important preparation and squad-building for both nations ahead of upcoming ICC events. Cricket enthusiasts should check their respective broadcasters and streaming platforms for the confirmed telecast details.
key_facts: Five-match T20I series between India and England in 2026
Full schedule, squads and venues confirmed
Live streaming and telecast details available for Indian fans

stories.headline: Preview: England vs India five-match T20I series — what to expect
stories.what: Cricbuzz has published a detailed preview of the five-match T20I series between England and India, examining the strengths, weaknesses and key matchups that will define the contest. The series is one of the most anticipated bilateral T20 encounters of the year, pitting England's power-hitting philosophy against India's all-round depth and home conditions. The preview sets the scene for what promises to be a fiercely competitive five-game battle.
stories.lens: A major T20 series between India and England is a must-watch event for Indian cricket fans, and understanding the form and matchups beforehand heightens the experience.
stories.article:
England and India are set to lock horns in a five-match T20I series, and by all accounts it should be one of the most competitive bilateral T20 contests of the year. Cricbuzz's preview highlights the tactical intrigue across departments as two cricketing powerhouses prepare to face off.

England come in with a well-established T20 identity — aggressive batting throughout the order and versatile bowling options — while India counter with a deep squad that blends experience with exciting young talent. The series will test both sides' adaptability across conditions and match situations.

Key individual matchups are expected to define the outcome, with batters, bowlers and all-rounders on both sides eager to stake their claims ahead of the next ICC T20 World Cup cycle. For India, in-form players will be under scrutiny, while England will be looking to consolidate their playing philosophy.

With five matches giving both teams room to experiment and recover from early setbacks, the series promises a fascinating arc over its duration. Cricket fans across India can look forward to an engrossing contest that will have implications for squad selection and rankings alike.
key_facts: Five T20Is between England and India
Series previewed as one of the most competitive bilateral T20 contests of the year
Both sides bring strong and contrasting T20 philosophies

stories.headline: Sunny Deol wanted Damini 2 for years, but his courtroom return arrives via Ikka
stories.what: Bollywood actor Sunny Deol has revealed that he made sustained efforts to bring about a sequel to his acclaimed 1993 courtroom drama 'Damini', but was unable to make it happen despite trying hard. His courtroom comeback eventually arrives through a different film, 'Ikka'. Deol's admission — 'We tried so much' — captures the weight of a project that clearly mattered to him personally. 'Damini', in which Deol delivered one of Hindi cinema's most beloved courtroom performances, remains a touchstone of 1990s Bollywood.
stories.lens: For a generation of Hindi film fans, Sunny Deol's 'Damini' monologue is cinema mythology — the news that he tried repeatedly for a sequel, and what eventually replaced it, is a story about creative longing that resonates beyond the industry.
stories.article:
Sunny Deol has revealed that he spent years trying to make 'Damini 2' a reality, only to eventually settle for a different courtroom drama — 'Ikka' — as his return to the genre that produced one of his career-defining performances.

'We tried so much,' Deol said, according to The Indian Express, in a candid admission that speaks to the long shadow 'Damini' has cast over his career. The 1993 film, directed by Rajkumar Santoshi, featured Deol as a passionate lawyer in a role that became iconic, its courtroom monologue entering the collective memory of an entire generation of Hindi film audiences.

The quest for a sequel is not unusual in Bollywood, but Deol's persistence over what appears to have been an extended period underscores how personally significant the property was to him. The fact that no sequel materialised — despite the film's lasting cultural resonance — reflects the complex rights, creative, and commercial hurdles that often stand between a beloved film and its follow-up.

'Ikka' now becomes the vehicle for Deol's courtroom return, offering him a fresh stage for the kind of theatrical intensity that made 'Damini' a landmark. Whether it can match the original's cultural impact remains to be seen, but its existence owes something to a pursuit that began three decades ago.
key_facts: Sunny Deol tried for years to make a Damini sequel
Sequel never materialised despite sustained efforts
Deol's courtroom return comes via new film 'Ikka'
Original 'Damini' (1993) directed by Rajkumar Santoshi

stories.headline: Horror film 'Obsession' gets OTT release in India: here's how to watch Clive Barker's blockbuster
stories.what: The horror film 'Obsession', associated with filmmaker Clive Barker, is now available to stream in India via OTT platforms, with NDTV Profit providing a guide on how Indian viewers can access it. The film's OTT arrival makes a major international horror title accessible to India's growing genre-film audience. Horror has been one of the fastest-growing segments on streaming platforms in India, with both international and domestic titles finding increasingly large audiences.
stories.lens: As Indian horror fandom grows rapidly on streaming platforms, the arrival of international genre films like this one signals how seriously global OTT players are taking the Indian horror audience.
stories.article:
The horror film 'Obsession', connected to acclaimed horror auteur Clive Barker, has made its OTT debut in India, with streaming access now available to Indian subscribers, according to NDTV Profit.

NDTV Profit has published a guide detailing how Indian viewers can watch the film, reflecting the growing appetite for international horror content on Indian streaming platforms. Barker is one of horror cinema and literature's most distinctive voices, known for creating visceral and imaginative worlds — most famously the 'Hellraiser' franchise — and his association with 'Obsession' has generated significant interest among genre fans.

The arrival of international horror titles via OTT has become a regular feature of India's streaming landscape, as platforms compete to offer content that caters to niche but intensely engaged audiences. Horror, in particular, has seen a notable surge in Indian viewership, driven partly by the success of domestic horror productions and partly by younger audiences exploring global genre cinema.

For Indian fans of Barker's work, the OTT release removes the accessibility barriers that have historically kept many international horror films out of reach. The convenience of streaming means that a film of this profile can find its Indian audience without requiring a theatrical run.
key_facts: Horror film 'Obsession' is now streaming in India on OTT
Film associated with horror filmmaker Clive Barker
NDTV Profit published a viewing guide for Indian audiences

stories.headline: South Indian cinema's most contentious moments of 2026 so far: Vijay's censor row to Drishyam 3
stories.what: The Times of India has compiled the major controversies that have rocked South Indian cinema in the first half of 2026, with two standout flashpoints: the censor certification dispute surrounding Vijay's 'Jana Nayagan', and developments around 'Drishyam 3'. The round-up reflects a period of unusual turbulence for the southern film industry, with certification battles, content disputes, and franchise decisions generating significant public and industry debate. Together, the controversies underscore how closely watched — and contested — South Indian cinema has become.
stories.lens: South Indian films now dominate national box offices and cultural conversation, so the controversies they generate — especially censor battles involving a star as influential as Vijay — matter to audiences well beyond the south.
stories.article:
South Indian cinema has been a hotbed of controversy in the opening months of 2026, with a series of disputes ranging from censor certification rows to franchise announcements generating debate both within the industry and among audiences nationwide, according to a Times of India round-up.

The most prominent flashpoint has been the censor row surrounding Vijay's 'Jana Nayagan'. The certification dispute drew national attention, with the film — starring one of Tamil cinema's biggest stars — caught in a stand-off that highlighted the ongoing tensions between filmmakers, certification authorities, and the political sensitivities that often inform such decisions in southern cinema. Vijay, who has recently stepped into active politics, finds his cinematic work under a sharper public lens than ever before.

'Drishyam 3' has also generated its share of intrigue and debate. The franchise, which has become one of Malayalam cinema's most celebrated modern properties, faces the considerable weight of audience expectations that come with continuing a critically and commercially acclaimed series.

The pattern of controversies across these six months points to a South Indian film industry operating at greater scale and visibility than at any previous point — which means that disputes, creative decisions, and certification battles are now national news rather than regional footnotes. With pan-India releases and massive OTT footprints, what happens in Chennai or Kochi reverberates in multiplexes and living rooms from Kashmir to Kanyakumari.
key_facts: Vijay's 'Jana Nayagan' faced a censor certification dispute
'Drishyam 3' is among the South Indian controversies of 2026
Round-up covers the first half of 2026
Compiled by Times of India

stories.headline: Ahmed Khan reveals why Sanjay Dutt quit Welcome To The Jungle mid-shoot
stories.what: Director Ahmed Khan has broken his silence on why Bollywood veteran Sanjay Dutt walked off the sets of 'Welcome To The Jungle' after having already begun filming. Khan's revelation sheds light on what became one of the more talked-about behind-the-scenes fallouts in recent Hindi cinema. The departure forced a significant shake-up in the film's cast and production plans. The reasons cited by Khan point to creative or personal differences that proved irreconcilable.
stories.lens: When a star of Sanjay Dutt's calibre quits a big-ticket comedy franchise mid-shoot, it signals the kind of behind-the-scenes friction that can reshape an entire film's release — and audiences deserve to know why.
stories.article:
Director Ahmed Khan has publicly explained why Sanjay Dutt exited 'Welcome To The Jungle' after having already started shooting for the film — a departure that sent ripples through the production and forced a rethink of the ensemble cast.

Khan, who helmed the big-budget comedy, revealed the circumstances in an interview with The Indian Express, offering the clearest account yet of one of Bollywood's more surprising mid-production exits. The film, part of the long-running 'Welcome' franchise, was already a crowded production featuring a large ensemble, and Dutt's exit added an unexpected layer of complication to its already ambitious scope.

The 'Welcome' franchise has been a reliable crowd-puller, and the third instalment was expected to raise the scale significantly. Dutt's involvement had been a much-publicised selling point, making his departure all the more jarring for fans and trade observers. Productions of this size carry enormous financial stakes, and a star exit mid-shoot typically demands reshoots, renegotiations, and creative restructuring.

Khan's willingness to speak openly about the episode is notable, as Bollywood productions rarely air internal conflicts publicly. The revelation adds context to months of speculation that followed Dutt's exit, and underscores the complex logistical and personal negotiations that define big Hindi film productions.
key_facts: Sanjay Dutt exited 'Welcome To The Jungle' after beginning shoot
Director Ahmed Khan revealed the reasons behind the departure
The film is part of the long-running 'Welcome' franchise
The Indian Express published Khan's account

stories.headline: Flautist Rakesh Chaurasia on his love for Bollywood and keeping Indian classical music alive on screen
stories.what: Renowned flautist Rakesh Chaurasia, nephew of the legendary Hariprasad Chaurasia, has spoken about his enduring love for composing and performing for Bollywood, in an interview with The Times of India. Chaurasia — one of India's foremost flute virtuosos — reflects on how film music continues to offer him a platform to bring the bansuri to a mass audience, keeping an ancient classical instrument at the heart of contemporary popular culture. His comments offer a window into the relationship between Indian classical music and mainstream cinema.
stories.lens: In an era when Bollywood soundtracks are dominated by electronic beats, a master flautist's insistence on the relevance of the bansuri is a quiet but important cultural statement.
stories.article:
Rakesh Chaurasia, one of India's finest flautists and a torchbearer of the Chaurasia musical legacy, has declared his deep and abiding love for playing for Bollywood — positioning film music not as a commercial detour but as a genuine creative calling.

In an interview with The Times of India, Chaurasia spoke about what draws him to Hindi film music despite his stature as a classical musician. The bansuri, which he plays at the highest concert level, finds a different but equally meaningful expression in film compositions, he suggested — reaching listeners who might never attend a classical recital.

Chaurasia is the nephew of Pandit Hariprasad Chaurasia, whose flute has defined the sonic texture of Indian classical and film music for decades. Rakesh has built a substantial career of his own, contributing to numerous film soundtracks while maintaining a rigorous classical performance schedule. His presence in Bollywood studios has helped ensure that the flute retains a voice in an industry increasingly drawn to synthesised and electronic sounds.

His comments arrive at a moment when Indian film music is undergoing rapid transformation, with fewer orchestral recordings and a growing reliance on digital production. In that context, a master instrumentalist's enthusiasm for Bollywood carries a significance beyond personal preference — it is, in a quiet way, a case for preserving the living craft of acoustic music in Indian cinema.
key_facts: Rakesh Chaurasia is a leading Indian flautist
Nephew of legendary Pandit Hariprasad Chaurasia
Spoke about his love for performing for Bollywood
Interview published in Times of India
